that's working, like if one list doesn't work, let's say you try it twice and it doesn't work, uh, get rid of it
for that offer. So one thing to note is if you have multiple offers, some list will work for one offer but it
might not work for another offer. So let's say you have a testosterone offer and then you have a weight
loss offer. Maybe the weight loss offer works that list, but the testosterone one doesn't. So then you
kind of keep that in mind for your future ones and you only promote the weight loss one on that one.
Um, that's another way to have kind of keeping, um, the email list from dying out.
Speaker 1 (36:08):
If you have multiple offers that you can run even better. So that way you have multiple creatives,
multiple offers, multiple subject lines, and you can really just, uh, keep rotating them all. Um, yeah, one
big thing here is know what numbers you want to hit. People always ask me like, what, what kind of
return our ROI I need to look for? And honestly, it's all up to the business owner. Uh, some business
owners are super conservative and they don't want anything less than making a profit on the front end.
Some are okay with breaking even. Some are okay with losing 30%. Some are okay with losing 50%
because they know they can make it up on the backend. It really comes down to the business owner and
kind of their risk tolerance and how well they know their backend. Uh, pretty much all it comes down to,
like I said, some people, we always kind of did ours we would shoot to kind of break even, but we would
go down to, I think about 30% negative 30% ROI is kind of about the lowest we would go. Um, but kind
of looking back now, like as good as a lot of these customers where we probably would have been
smarter to go negative 50% because we did make it all up within the next, got a couple of months. So
that's the one thing where I really got to know your numbers and you have to know exactly how much
those customers are worth to you in the long run. Uh, so you can figure out how much to pay for them.
